name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-18
title: Assembled prompt's bash 'trap EXIT' for scope marker cleanup does not work across Bash tool calls
description: dispatch-tdd-subagent.py Step 3 LOCK (lines 353-358) instructs the subagent: touch <marker>; trap 'rm -f <marker>' EXIT. Step 9 UNLOCK says 'Scope marker removed automatically by trap on EXIT'. But a trap registered inside one Bash tool call only fires when THAT bash process exits — i.e., immediately after the touch command completes (within milliseconds). All subsequent Bash() tool calls run in fresh shell processes with no trap and no marker, so they will hit scope-guard DENY since LOCK has effectively self-unlocked. This is a fundamental misunderstanding of how Claude's Bash tool sessions work. Fix: drop the trap and instead instruct the subagent to explicitly 'rm -f <marker>' in Step 9 UNLOCK.

history.note: Fixed as part of Wave 1 bundle in PR (BUG-15/18/22/28/29 all addressed by dispatch-tdd-subagent.py prompt edits).
